channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft smashes Patch Tuesday record for second successive month
item.description: Vulnerability counts have been surging this year, and Microsoft's mammoth disclosure this week of 622 bugs is larger than the three previous months combined.

item.title: [Faible] SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description:
For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up.
Enterprise workflows now live across SaaS…

item.title: [Critique] Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description:
Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive.
It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of…

item.title: [Faible] New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech
item.description:
A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages.
This on-demand webinar reveals how this…

item.title: [Critique] Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
item.description: Open a repository in Cursor on Windows and, if a file named git.exe is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. No click, no approval dialog, no warning that anything in the folder is about to…

item.title: [Moyen] CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
item.description:
Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity.
The affected packages are…

item.title: [Critique] First VPN santionné par la justice américaine
item.description: Un VPN connu et deux internautes accusés de soutenir des groupes de rançongiciel.

item.title: [Faible] Doxbin : un faux administrateur condamné au pays de Galles
item.description: Un jeune internaute condamné pour swatting avait usurpé l’identité du véritable administrateur d'un site pirate.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft: Some Dell PCs shut down after recent Windows updates
item.description: Microsoft is blocking this month's Windows 11 security updates on some Dell devices because they are causing shutdowns and performance issues. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Un hébergeur russe au cœur d’un réseau cybercriminel
item.description: Washington accuse la société Media Land d’avoir fourni l’infrastructure ayant soutenu rançongiciels, phishing et fraude.

item.title: [Critique] US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
item.description: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims…

item.title: [Critique] Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
item.description:
SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution.
The…

item.title: [Critique] SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
item.description: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates.…

item.title: [Critique] Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
item.description: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its Security Update…

item.title: [Élevé] Spanish Police take down €140 million cyber fraud ring, arrest four
item.description: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]

item.title: [Faible] US unseals indictment against alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
item.description: The Russians face multiple charges for allegedly providing cybercriminals with infrastructure and tech support through the St. Petersburg-based business Media Land and a sister company, ML Cloud.

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its…

item.title: [Faible] Nearly 300 GitHub repos pose as legit software to push malware
item.description: A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update, which includes the July 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for 570 vulnerabilities, along with additional security fixes. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] SAP Patches CVSS 9.9 NetWeaver ABAP Flaw That Could Expose or Modify Data
item.description:
SAP has rolled out updates to address multiple vulnerabilities as part of its July 2026 security updates, including a critical flaw in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP.
The vulnerability in question is…

item.title: [Critique] Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days
item.description: Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Windows 11 KB5101650 & KB5099414 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5101650 and KB5099414 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Finland issues wanted notice for hacker behind massive psychotherapy data breach
item.description: The defendant's lawyer told Finnish media that he does not know where his client is but believes Kivimäki is outside Finland.

item.title: [Faible] Researchers Say Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Rogue Extensions Trigger Gmail Reads
item.description:
Any other browser extension that can run a script on claude.ai can still trigger Claude for Chrome tasks aimed at your Gmail, your latest Google Doc and its comments, and your Calendar.
Both this and ClaudeBleed need a…

item.title: [Faible] LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments.
"LabubaRAT creates a…

item.title: [Critique] Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
item.description: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch the flaw. [...]

item.title: [Faible] LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
item.description: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]

item.title: [Faible] You Don't Have to Run an Exploit to Know If You're Vulnerable
item.description: Many vulnerabilities cannot be safely validated with live exploits, either because no exploit exists or the affected systems are too critical to test. Picus explains how TTP chaining helps organizations determine…

item.title: [Élevé] NATO logistics, Ukrainian troops are top subjects of Russian camera hacks, advisory says
item.description: Dutch intelligence officials report that at least one Russian agency is compromising internet-connected cameras across Europe to spy on military logistics and Ukrainian personnel.

item.title: [Faible] RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two access control-related flaws impacting the RabbitMQ message broker service that could allow attackers to leak OAuth client secrets, expose enterprise messaging…
